what_it_does: A systemd service unit that launches the OpenClaw Gateway Node.js binary with the gateway subcommand bound to port 18789, sets restart behavior, environment variables, and an optional per-user secrets file.
observations: ExecStart uses a hardcoded linuxbrew Node path and /home/linuxbrew/.linuxbrew/lib/node_modules/openclaw/dist/index.js
The command runs the gateway subcommand with --port 18789
Restart=always with RestartSec=5 and 30-second start/stop timeouts
EnvironmentFile=-%h/company/.secrets makes a user-local secrets file optional
PATH environment lists many Node/runtime version managers (nvm, volta, asdf, bun, fnm, pnpm)
Service metadata environment variables include OPENCLAW_SERVICE_KIND, OPENCLAW_SERVICE_VERSION, OPENCLAW_SERVICE_MARKER, and OPENCLAW_WINDOWS_TASK_NAME
HOME is set to /home/tim and TMPDIR to /tmp
NODE_EXTRA_CA_CERTS points to the system CA bundle
standards: Extensive PATH enumerating many Node version managers
A Windows task-name environment variable inside a Linux systemd unit
conventions: Follows systemd unit file syntax with [Unit], [Service], and [Install] sections
Uses %h specifier for user home in EnvironmentFile
Restart=always and control-group kill mode are common service resilience patterns
concerns: Hardcoded absolute paths assume a specific linuxbrew and user-home layout
No explicit User= or WorkingDirectory directive in the file
Optional secrets file path is relative to the invoking user's home
No ExecReload or explicit logging configuration
Version string appears in both Description and environment variable and could drift
notes: This is a user-scoped systemd service that starts the OpenClaw Gateway Node app on port 18789 with a long custom PATH and an optional secrets file.
questions: Which user account is this unit intended to run under?
Is the linuxbrew Node path guaranteed to exist on the deployment host?
What credentials or values does %h/company/.secrets contain?
Is port 18789 exposed externally or used only locally?
Why is OPENCLAW_WINDOWS_TASK_NAME defined in a Linux systemd unit?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the content: the Description identifies an OpenClaw Gateway and ExecStart runs the gateway subcommand on port 18789.
apparent_themes: Node.js runtime discovery via many version managers
intention_signals: To provide a resilient, auto-restarting systemd service for the OpenClaw Gateway with a broad PATH for locating Node/runtime tools and optional per-user secrets.
novelty: The combination of a Linux systemd unit with a Windows task-name environment variable and a PATH that enumerates numerous Node version managers is unusual.
contribution: Defines the systemd service contract for OpenClaw Gateway, including the launch command, port, restart policy, environment setup, metadata variables, and optional secrets file, so a fused system can reproduce how the service is supervised on a Linux host.
summary_for_embedding: A systemd service unit file that starts the OpenClaw Gateway Node.js application on port 18789 with a restart policy, custom PATH, metadata environment variables, and an optional user secrets file.
intention_for_embedding: To register and supervise the OpenClaw Gateway process under systemd with environment setup and automatic restart.
